programming_intro.label: Programming Languages: Talking to Computers
programming_intro.summary: Learn what a programming language is, why computers need precise instructions, and why Python is a great first language.

intro.title: Programming languages help humans give instructions to computers.
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Just like people communicate using language, we communicate with computers using **programming languages**.

A programming language lets us write instructions in a form a computer can follow.

There are many programming languages, such as:

- Python
- Java
- C

Each language has its own style and strengths.

In this course, we focus on **Python** because it is beginner-friendly, widely used, and great for learning core programming ideas.

So the big idea is:

> a programming language is a way to tell a computer what to do

m0_prog_language_definition.title: Programming language
m0_prog_language_definition.prompt: A **programming language** is best described as:

help.concept: A programming language is a way to give instructions to a computer.
help.hint_1: Think about what lets humans tell a computer what to do.
help.hint_2: It is not hardware or a browser. It is the language used to write instructions.

m0_prog_language_definition.hint: We use programming languages to give computers instructions.

options.a: A way to give instructions to a computer

m0_prog_python_is_language.title: Python

help.concept: Python is a programming language used to write programs.
help.hint_1: Think about what category Python belongs to.
help.hint_2: It is not a hardware brand or shortcut. It is a language for writing code.

m0_prog_python_is_language.hint: Python is a language used to write programs.

m0_prog_instructions_precise.title: Precision matters
m0_prog_instructions_precise.prompt: Computers usually need instructions that are:

help.concept: Computers follow exact instructions, so code must be clear and precise.
help.hint_2: A computer does not infer meaning the way humans often do. It follows what you wrote.

options.a: Vague so they can guess what you mean
